title: Stand Up India — ₹10L-₹1Cr Loans for SC/ST/Women Entrepreneurs
meta_description: Stand Up India 2026 — bank loans ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore for SC/ST and women entrepreneurs to start businesses. Eligibility, interest rate, how to apply.

hero.one_liner: Bank loans from ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore specifically for SC/ST and women entrepreneurs to start greenfield enterprises

what_is_it: Stand Up India is a flagship scheme launched on 5 April 2016 by the Ministry of Finance to promote entrepreneurship among Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), and Women. The scheme mandates that every bank branch in India must give at least ONE loan between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore to an SC/ST borrower AND at least ONE loan to a Woman borrower for setting up a new (greenfield) enterprise. This ensures reserved quota of credit for underrepresented entrepreneurs.

This guarantee of loan availability means there are at least 2.5 lakh potential loans available across India's 1.25 lakh+ bank branches every year. The loans cover both manufacturing and services sectors — you can start a factory, a restaurant, a salon, a coaching center, a diagnostic lab, a transport business, or any other new enterprise. Over 2.3 lakh loans worth ₹40,000+ crore have been sanctioned since launch.

The scheme is facilitated through the Stand Up India portal (standupmitra.in) which connects aspiring entrepreneurs with bank branches. The portal provides handholding support including business plan preparation, mentoring, connecting with existing entrepreneurs, and linking with industry experts. Government also provides credit guarantee through CGTMSE to reduce collateral requirements.

Remarkably, 81% of Stand Up India loans have gone to women entrepreneurs, making it one of the most successful women's entrepreneurship programs globally. Combined with government skill training schemes (PMKVY, PM Vishwakarma), Stand Up India has enabled over 2 lakh entrepreneurs to formalize and scale their businesses.

eligibility.label: Who can apply
eligibility.value: SC/ST individuals AND/OR Women (18+ years) — any combination that includes at least one SC/ST or woman borrower

eligibility.label: Enterprise type
eligibility.value: Greenfield enterprise only — new business, NOT expansion of existing business

eligibility.label: Shareholding
eligibility.value: SC/ST and/or woman entrepreneur must hold at least 51% shareholding and controlling stake

eligibility.label: Borrower history
eligibility.value: Must not be a defaulter on any bank loan. Enterprise should not be already funded by another government scheme.

eligibility.label: Loan amount
eligibility.value: ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore — composite loan covering term loan + working capital

eligibility.label: Collateral
eligibility.value: Up to ₹10 lakh — no collateral needed. Above ₹10 lakh — bank may ask for collateral but CGTMSE guarantee cover is available (government backs 85% of loan)

eligibility.label: Repayment
eligibility.value: Maximum 7 years with a moratorium period of up to 18 months (no EMI during moratorium)

extra_sections.heading: Business Types Eligible for Stand Up India
rows: Food processing, garment factory, handicrafts, printing press, packaging unit, furniture, agro-processing, herbal products, spice grinding
Coaching center, diagnostic lab, pharmacy, beauty salon, restaurant, day care, event management, IT services, travel agency
Wholesale distribution, retail store, e-commerce business, franchise operations
Cab/taxi business, small bus operation, logistics, food delivery network
Guest house, home stay, restaurant, catering business
Software development, digital marketing agency, web design, app development

extra_sections.heading: How Stand Up India Loans Work — The Complete Package
content:
Loan Structure

The Stand Up India loan is a COMPOSITE loan — it covers both your term loan (for buying assets like machinery, equipment, furniture, vehicle) AND working capital (for running expenses like raw materials, rent, salaries for initial months). Maximum loan: ₹1 crore (combined term + working capital).
Minimum: ₹10 lakh.

Margin Money Requirement

You need to bring at least 10% of the project cost as your own margin money — the bank covers the remaining 90%. Example: For ₹50 lakh project, you invest ₹5 lakh, bank lends ₹45 lakh.
This ensures you have skin in the game.

Interest Rate Structure

Interest rate: Bank's standard lending rate (MCLR + spread). Typically 10-12% per annum depending on bank and loan amount.
This is similar to regular business loan rates. The advantage of Stand Up India is GUARANTEED access to credit, not subsidized interest.
CGTMSE guarantee reduces bank risk, so they lend readily without demanding heavy collateral.

Repayment Terms

Maximum repayment period: 7 years. Maximum moratorium (interest-only period, no principal EMI): 18 months.
Example: Borrow ₹50 lakh. First 18 months: pay only interest (~₹5 lakh/year).
Months 19-84: pay EMI covering principal + interest. This gives your business 1.5 years to stabilize before principal repayment starts.

Credit Guarantee from CGTMSE

Government provides Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) cover — government guarantees 85% of the loan. This means if you default, CGTMSE pays the bank 85% of the outstanding amount.
This drastically reduces bank's risk and makes them willing to lend to first-time entrepreneurs without demanding land/property as collateral.

steps.title: Register on standupmitra.in
steps.description: Visit standupmitra.in and click 'Register as Aspiring Entrepreneur'. Fill in your details: category (SC/ST/Woman), proposed business activity, loan amount needed, and state.

steps.title: Get matched with a bank branch
steps.description: The portal matches you with the nearest bank branch that has Stand Up India quota available. Every bank branch is mandated to give at least one SC/ST and one woman loan per year. You can also directly approach any bank branch — the manager will help you apply for Stand Up India.

steps.title: Prepare business plan with free support
steps.description: The portal offers free handholding — help with preparing a detailed business plan, financial projections, and loan application. You can also get support from SIDBI's Support Centers, NABARD's LEDP (Livelihood and Enterprise Development Programme), or your local DIC (District Industries Centre) — all free.

steps.title: Submit loan application at matched bank
steps.description: Visit the matched bank branch with your business plan, KYC documents (Aadhaar, PAN), caste certificate (for SC/ST if applicable), and enterprise details. The bank evaluates your plan, conducts site visit (for manufacturing), and sanctions the loan typically within 30-60 days.

steps.title: Loan disbursed in stages
steps.description: Term loan (for equipment/machinery/vehicle) is disbursed upfront or in stages based on asset purchase. Working capital is credited to your business account. Start your enterprise and begin repaying as per the agreed schedule after the moratorium period.

extra_sections.heading: Comparison with Other Business Loan Schemes
rows: Up to ₹10L: None. Above: CGTMSE guarantee covers 85%

extra_sections.heading: How to Boost Loan Chances & Get Higher Amount
content:
Strong Business Plan

The most critical factor. Your business plan must clearly show: Market demand for your product/service, your competitive advantage, 3-year financial projections showing profitability, risk mitigation strategy.
Use the template provided on standupmitra.in or get help from a DIC consultant.

Track Record Helps

If you've previously run a successful business (even small), mention it. Experience in the sector you're starting in is a strong positive.
If first-time entrepreneur, highlight any relevant training (government skill courses, apprenticeships, industry experience).

Co-applicant Strengthens Application

A business partner with contrasting skills (one technical, one business/marketing) shows thought-out structure. Two co-applicants (one SC/ST, one Woman) can apply together and likely get higher loan amount.

Collateral Helps Get Lower Interest

Even small collateral (like a property, vehicle, or standing crop insurance) can reduce the interest rate by 0.5-1% and speed up approval. CGTMSE guarantee reduces this need, but collateral still helps.

Government Training Certificates

Having completed government skill training (PMKVY, NSDC, or state training programs) in your field strengthens your credibility. Many banks give preference to trained candidates.

extra_sections.heading: Post-Loan Support from Government
content:
After your Stand Up India loan is approved, you're not left alone. Government provides ongoing support:

GST Registration & Compliance Help

Free GST registration and basic compliance training through your bank or DIC.

Accounting Software Access

Many states provide subsidized or free accounting software (like Busy, Tally) for registered enterprises.

Insurance Schemes

Access to PMRPY (Pradhan Mantri Rashtriya Swasthyashwi Yojana) for employee health insurance at heavily subsidized rates.

Export Support

If business involves exports, Export-Import (Exim) bank offers refinancing on Stand Up India loans at lower rates.

Follow-up Mentoring

SIDBI supports many branches with follow-up mentoring to help entrepreneurs navigate challenges in Year 1-2.

how_to_apply.title: Register on standupmitra.in
how_to_apply.description: Enter your category (SC/ST/Woman), business details, and loan amount.

how_to_apply.title: Get matched with a bank branch
how_to_apply.description: Portal connects you with nearest bank with Stand Up India quota.

how_to_apply.title: Prepare business plan (free support available)
how_to_apply.description: Use portal template or DIC/SIDBI support. Include financial projections.

how_to_apply.title: Submit application at bank
how_to_apply.description: Visit matched branch with plan, KYC, caste certificate. Bank typically sanctions within 30-60 days.

how_to_apply.title: Loan disbursed, start business
how_to_apply.description: Repay as per schedule. Usually 18-month moratorium period before principal EMI starts.

important_alert.text: Every bank branch is MANDATED to give Stand Up India loans. If a bank refuses your application without valid reason, escalate to the bank's Regional Manager or file a complaint on standupmitra.in. You can also approach the District Lead Bank. Don't accept refusal without trying escalation.

important_dates.label: Application
important_dates.value: Open throughout the year at all bank branches and standupmitra.in

important_dates.label: Processing time
important_dates.value: 30-60 days for loan sanction after complete application submission

faqs.question: I'm a general category woman. Am I eligible?
faqs.answer: Yes! Women of ALL categories (General, OBC, SC, ST) are eligible for Stand Up India. The scheme has two beneficiary groups: (1) SC/ST individuals (male or female), AND (2) ALL women regardless of category. A general category woman entrepreneur is fully eligible and many banks prioritize women loans.

faqs.question: Can two people apply jointly?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can have a partnership, LLP, or company structure, but the SC/ST and/or woman applicant must hold at least 51% shareholding and must be the controlling partner/director. For example, a woman can partner with her husband, but she must hold majority stake. This ensures the benefit goes to the intended category.

faqs.question: I already run a small business. Can I apply?
faqs.answer: No — Stand Up India is only for greenfield (brand new) enterprises. If you already run a business and want expansion funding, consider PM Mudra Yojana (up to ₹10 lakh) or regular business loans. However, if you're starting a completely new, separate business (even if you've had another business before), you can apply.

faqs.question: What if the bank rejects my application?
faqs.answer: Ask for written reasons for rejection. Common reasons: poor CIBIL score (loan repayment history), insufficient business plan, or the branch has already exhausted its Stand Up India quota for that year. If rejected unfairly, escalate to the bank's Zonal Manager, contact standupmitra.in for support, or approach the District Lead Bank Manager. Don't give up after first rejection.

faqs.question: Is there any subsidy or grant component?
faqs.answer: No — Stand Up India is purely a LOAN scheme with no grant or subsidy. You must repay the full amount with interest (typically 10-12%). The benefits are: (1) Guaranteed access to credit (which SC/ST and women entrepreneurs often struggle to get from banks), and (2) CGTMSE guarantee cover (government backs 85%, reducing collateral needs). No interest subsidy.

faqs.question: After I get the loan, what if my business fails?
faqs.answer: If your business doesn't succeed and you can't repay, the bank can use CGTMSE guarantee to recover 85% of the outstanding loan. You're liable for the remaining 15%. This is why it's critical to have a solid business plan and backup strategy. However, banks also show flexibility to restructure loans (reduce EMI, extend tenure) if you proactively communicate when facing difficulties.

faqs.question: Can I use Stand Up India loan for opening a franchise?
faqs.answer: Yes, if it's your first-time franchise ownership (greenfield). For example, opening a Domino's franchise, a beauty salon franchise, or a coaching center franchise. The franchise company must be established and approved by the bank. You must have at least 51% ownership as the primary entrepreneur (SC/ST/Woman).
